attacks.name: delete (mid-chain)
attacks.note: the successor's prev_hash no longer links to its new predecessor; verifier names the first orphaned event
attacks.threat: remove one event from the middle of the log

attacks.name: reorder (swap two events)
attacks.note: both swapped positions' prev_hash linkage break
attacks.threat: swap two adjacent events to change ordering

attacks.name: forge (integrity field content_hash)
attacks.note: tampering the hashed field breaks the successor's prev_hash
attacks.threat: rewrite the hashed content_hash of one event

attacks.name: forge (payload only)
attacks.note: GAP: verify_event_chain does not recompute content_hash from the payload, so a payload-only rewrite is not caught. Mitigations mnemo ships: the memory record's content is hash-bound (verify_chain recomputes it); Postgres' prevent_event_modification trigger blocks in-place UPDATE
attacks.threat: rewrite an event's payload JSON, leaving content_hash intact

attacks.name: truncate (tail)
attacks.note: GAP: the surviving prefix is a valid chain; a pure verifier has no expected length/head anchor. Mitigations mnemo ships: a signed checkpoint records the expected latest hash + count; Postgres append-only trigger blocks tail deletion
attacks.threat: drop the last k events from the log
